message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’ve noted your update that you won’t be able to pay this month. Given the hardship already indicated, what month do you expect your income or ability to pay may resume?
I’m unemployed right now, so I don’t know when that will be and I’m not promising a payment.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I understand you’re unemployed and not able to commit to a payment right now. I’ve noted that hardship update. Would you like this routed for hardship review?
You can route it if you want, but I’m still not agreeing to any payment.

Understood. I’ve noted that you are unemployed, not agreeing to any payment, and that hardship routing is appropriate. We will route this for hardship review and won’t ask for a payment commitment here.

A stop-contact flag is already present in case context, so Agent 2 should avoid repeating assessment questions or requesting payment commitments and should continue naturally with hardship-review handling only, without restarting the conversation."}

Fine, route it, but I’m not making any payment promise.

Understood. I’ll route this for hardship review with no payment commitment recorded from you. At this stage, no extension beyond 30 days can be offered, and any reduced closure or payment plan would require policy-compliant approval after review.
That doesn’t change anything—I still can’t pay and I’m not committing to anything.
Understood. I’ve noted that you cannot pay and are not making any payment commitment. I’m marking this for hardship review and no payment arrangement will be recorded from this conversation.
